---
title: "MTTR vs. MOVA"
subtitle: "Why MTTR Alone Misleads Security Teams"
author: "Caleb Kinney"
date: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from datetime import datetime

import pandas as pd
import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT, loc, style
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_blank,
    element_line,
    element_rect,
    element_text,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_color_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

metrics = pl.read_parquet("data/metrics.parquet")
summary = pl.read_parquet("data/summary.parquet")

STRATEGY_LABELS = {
    "oldest_first": "Oldest-First",
    "newest_first": "Newest-First",
}
METRIC_COLORS = {"MTTR": "#1F6F78", "MOVA": "#C65A21"}
STRATEGY_COLORS = {
    "Oldest-First": "#1F6F78",
    "Newest-First": "#8B1E3F",
}
ROOM_BG = "#f6f0e5"
PANEL_BG = "#fffaf2"
GRID_COLOR = "#d9cfbf"


def fmt_days(value: float) -> str:
    return f"{value:.1f}"


metrics_labeled = metrics.with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy_label"),
    pl.col("month").dt.strftime("%Y-%m").alias("month_label"),
)

plot_metrics = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        pl.col("mttr_days").alias("MTTR"),
        pl.col("mova_days").alias("MOVA"),
    )
    .unpivot(
        index=["strategy", "month_index"],
        on=["MTTR", "MOVA"],
        variable_name="metric",
        value_name="days",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

newest_plot = plot_metrics[plot_metrics["strategy"] == "Newest-First"].copy()
oldest_plot = plot_metrics[plot_metrics["strategy"] == "Oldest-First"].copy()

tail_plot = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        "aged_over_180",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

summary_display = (
    summary
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("Strategy"),
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1).alias("MTTR (days)"),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1).alias("MOVA (days)"),
        pl.col("aged_over_180").alias("180+ days"),
    )
    .select("Strategy", "MTTR (days)", "MOVA (days)", "open_count", "180+ days")
    .rename({"open_count": "Open"})
)

card_rows = (
    summary
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy"),
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1),
    )
    .sort("strategy", descending=True)
    .select("strategy", "mttr_days", "mova_days", "open_count", "aged_over_180")
    .to_dicts()
)

card_lookup = {row["strategy"]: row for row in card_rows}

shared_theme = (
    theme_minimal(base_size=22)
    + theme(
        figure_size=(11.2, 5.25),
        legend_position="top",
        legend_title=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_line(color=GRID_COLOR, size=0.55),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill=PANEL_BG, color=None),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill=ROOM_BG, color=None),
        strip_background=element_rect(fill="#eadfce", color="#eadfce"),
        strip_text=element_text(weight="bold", color="#172026"),
        axis_title=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        axis_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_title=element_text(weight="bold", color="#101828"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(color="#55606B", margin={"b": 10}),
        legend_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_margin=0.16,
    )
)

chart_theme = shared_theme + theme(
    legend_position="none",
    axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 10}),
    axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 10}),
)

tail_theme = shared_theme + theme(
    legend_position="none",
    axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 10}),
    axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 10}),
)


def strategy_chart(source: pd.DataFrame, title: str, subtitle: str):
    labels = source[source["month_index"] == source["month_index"].max()].copy()
    labels["label"] = labels.apply(
        lambda row: f'{row["metric"]} {row["days"]:.0f}d', axis=1
    )

    return (
        ggplot(source, aes(x="month_index", y="days", color="metric"))
        + geom_line(size=2.35)
        + geom_point(
            data=labels,
            size=3.8,
        )
        + geom_text(
            data=labels,
            mapping=aes(label="label"),
            ha="left",
            nudge_x=0.45,
            size=10,
        )
        + scale_color_manual(values=METRIC_COLORS)
        + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
        + labs(
            title=title,
            subtitle=subtitle,
            x="Month",
            y="Days",
        )
        + chart_theme
    )


tail_labels = tail_plot[tail_plot["month_index"] == tail_plot["month_index"].max()].copy()
tail_labels["label"] = tail_labels.apply(
    lambda row: f'{row["strategy"]} {row["aged_over_180"]}', axis=1
)
```

## MTTR doubled {.center .hook-slide}

:::: {.shock-card}
::: {.shock-label}
Mean time to remediate
:::

:::: {.shock-row}
::: {.shock-panel}
::: {.shock-caption}
Last month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
15d
:::
:::

::: {.shock-arrow}
→
:::

::: {.shock-panel .danger}
::: {.shock-caption}
This month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
31d
:::
:::
::::

::: {.shock-subtitle}
Most dashboards would call this failure before asking a better question.
:::
::::

---

## Why dashboards panic

::: {.incremental}
- Something slowed down
- The team got worse
- Leadership should worry
- Somebody needs to explain the red number
:::

---

## The twist {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR can rise

while risk drops
:::

::: {.kicker .centered}
Same team. Same capacity. Better prioritization.
:::

---

## Metrics shape behavior

::: {.callout}
Dashboards do not lie. They omit professionally.
:::

If the only visible win is lower MTTR, teams will chase lower MTTR.

# Start With MTTR {.center .section-slide}

## MTTR is flow

Time to remediate **closed** vulnerabilities.

::: {.kicker}
Closed work only.
:::

---

## What MTTR answers

::: {.metric-mid}
How long did the work we finished take?
:::

It is useful for spotting friction in completed remediation work.

---

## What MTTR misses

::: {.incremental}
- What is still open
- How old the open backlog is
- Whether risk is shrinking right now
:::

---

## Keep this line {.center}

::: {.metric-mid .centered}
MTTR measures completed work,

not current exposure.
:::

# Add MOVA {.center .section-slide}

## MOVA shows open risk

::: {.metric-mid}
**Mean Open Vulnerability Age**
:::

How old is the risk we still carry?

---

## Flow vs. stock

::: {.definition-grid}
<div class="definition-card">
  <div class="eyebrow">Flow</div>
  <h3>MTTR</h3>
  <p>How fast are we closing finished work?</p>
</div>
<div class="definition-card">
  <div class="eyebrow">Stock</div>
  <h3>MOVA</h3>
  <p>How old is the backlog we still own?</p>
</div>
:::

---

## Averages hide the tail

::: {.tail-example-grid}
<div class="tail-card">
  <div class="tail-number">95</div>
  <div class="tail-text">findings at 7 days</div>
</div>
<div class="tail-card">
  <div class="tail-number">5</div>
  <div class="tail-text">findings at 400 days</div>
</div>
<div class="tail-card emphasis">
  <div class="tail-text strong">The average looks fine.</div>
  <div class="tail-text">The tail does not.</div>
</div>
:::

---

## Report the bundle

::: {.signal-grid}
<div class="signal-card">
  <div class="signal-name">MTTR</div>
  <div class="signal-meaning">Closure speed</div>
</div>
<div class="signal-card">
  <div class="signal-name">MOVA</div>
  <div class="signal-meaning">Current exposure age</div>
</div>
<div class="signal-card">
  <div class="signal-name">Open Count</div>
  <div class="signal-meaning">Backlog size</div>
</div>
<div class="signal-card">
  <div class="signal-name">180+ Days</div>
  <div class="signal-meaning">Tail risk</div>
</div>
:::

# Same Data, Different Question {.center .section-slide}

## The model is boring on purpose

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false

vulns = pl.DataFrame({
    "id": [101, 102, 103],
    "created_at": [
        datetime(2026, 1, 10),
        datetime(2026, 2, 5),
        datetime(2026, 3, 1),
    ],
    "resolved_at": [
        datetime(2026, 2, 1),
        None,
        None,
    ],
})
```

---

## Same data: MTTR {auto-animate="true"}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
mttr = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_not_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("resolved_at") - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("days_to_fix")
    )
    .select(pl.col("days_to_fix").mean().alias("mttr_days"))
)
```

---

## Same data: MOVA {auto-animate="true"}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
as_of = datetime(2026, 4, 1)

mova = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.lit(as_of) - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("open_age_days")
    )
    .select(pl.col("open_age_days").mean().alias("mova_days"))
)
```

---

## Pipeline to deck

::: {.pipeline-grid}
<div class="pipeline-card">
  <div class="pipeline-step">01</div>
  <div class="pipeline-title">Generate</div>
  <div class="pipeline-text"><code>scripts/01_generate_data.py</code></div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-card">
  <div class="pipeline-step">02</div>
  <div class="pipeline-title">Simulate</div>
  <div class="pipeline-text"><code>scripts/02_simulate_metrics.py</code></div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-card">
  <div class="pipeline-step">03</div>
  <div class="pipeline-title">Build Outputs</div>
  <div class="pipeline-text"><code>scripts/03_build_outputs.py</code></div>
</div>
<div class="pipeline-card">
  <div class="pipeline-step">04</div>
  <div class="pipeline-title">Render Deck</div>
  <div class="pipeline-text"><code>index.qmd</code> + Reveal.js</div>
</div>
:::

::: {.kicker}
Python writes parquet. Quarto renders charts inline. Reveal.js handles pacing. One live view is optional.
:::

# Hold Capacity Constant {.center .section-slide}

## The setup is boring on purpose

::: {.simulation-grid}
<div class="sim-card">
  <div class="sim-number">24</div>
  <div class="sim-label">months</div>
</div>
<div class="sim-card">
  <div class="sim-number">360</div>
  <div class="sim-label">starting backlog</div>
</div>
<div class="sim-card">
  <div class="sim-number">60</div>
  <div class="sim-label">new / month</div>
</div>
<div class="sim-card">
  <div class="sim-number">60</div>
  <div class="sim-label">fixed / month</div>
</div>
:::

---

## Only the order changes

::: {.strategy-grid}
<div class="strategy-card">
  <div class="eyebrow">Strategy A</div>
  <h3>Newest-First</h3>
  <p>Fix newest findings first.</p>
</div>
<div class="strategy-card">
  <div class="eyebrow">Strategy B</div>
  <h3>Oldest-First</h3>
  <p>Fix oldest findings first.</p>
</div>
:::

::: {.kicker}
Same arrivals. Same fix rate. Same backlog size. Only the order changes.
:::

---

## The loop is tiny

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
def simulate(strategy: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    for as_of in months:
        current = active_vulns(backlog, as_of)
        to_fix = select_to_fix(current, 60, strategy)
        backlog = resolve_vulns(backlog, to_fix["id"], as_of)
        rows.append({
            "mttr_days": compute_mttr(backlog, as_of),
            "mova_days": compute_mova(backlog, as_of),
        })
```

# What Happens {.center .section-slide}

## Newest-First wins MTTR

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart for newest-first prioritization. MTTR stays near 15 days while MOVA rises above 900 days by month 24."
strategy_chart(
    newest_plot,
    title="Newest-First",
    subtitle="Low MTTR, steadily aging backlog",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
Fast closures. Old risk.
:::

---

## Oldest-First lowers exposure

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart for oldest-first prioritization. MTTR stays above 220 days while MOVA falls to roughly 92 days by month 24."
strategy_chart(
    oldest_plot,
    title="Oldest-First",
    subtitle="Higher MTTR, much younger backlog",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
Slower closures. Lower exposure.
:::

---

## Same work, different outcome

```{python}
#| echo: false
(
    GT(pd.DataFrame(summary_display.to_dicts()))
    .tab_header(
        title="After 24 months",
        subtitle="Same arrivals. Same fix capacity. Only prioritization changes.",
    )
    .tab_spanner(label="Flow", columns=["MTTR (days)"])
    .tab_spanner(label="Exposure", columns=["MOVA (days)", "Open", "180+ days"])
    .fmt_number(columns=["MTTR (days)", "MOVA (days)"], decimals=1)
    .data_color(columns=["MTTR (days)"], palette=["#E4F1F2", "#1F6F78"])
    .data_color(columns=["MOVA (days)", "180+ days"], palette=["#FFF0E8", "#8B1E3F"])
    .tab_style(
        style=style.fill(color="#f7f1e7"),
        locations=[loc.title(), loc.subtitle()],
    )
    .tab_source_note(source_note="MTTR alone rewards the strategy that leaves old risk behind.")
)
```

---

## The 180+ tail tells the truth

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart comparing the count of open vulnerabilities older than 180 days for oldest-first and newest-first prioritization."
(
    ggplot(tail_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="aged_over_180", color="strategy"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=tail_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_text(
        data=tail_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.45,
        size=10,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(values=STRATEGY_COLORS)
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
    + labs(
        title="180+ day backlog",
        subtitle="Newest-first keeps the tail alive",
        x="Month",
        y="Open vulnerabilities",
    )
    + tail_theme
)
```

---

## End state: the tail is the story

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
oldest = card_lookup["Oldest-First"]
newest = card_lookup["Newest-First"]

print(
    f"""
<div class="score-grid tail-grid">
  <div class="score-card score-good">
    <div class="score-title">Oldest-First</div>
    <div class="score-metric">{oldest["aged_over_180"]}</div>
    <div class="score-label">findings older than 180 days</div>
  </div>
  <div class="score-card score-bad">
    <div class="score-title">Newest-First</div>
    <div class="score-metric">{newest["aged_over_180"]}</div>
    <div class="score-label">findings older than 180 days</div>
  </div>
</div>
"""
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
Under newest-first, every open item is older than 180 days.
:::

---

## Leadership sees MTTR only {auto-animate="true"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
cards = []
for strategy in ["Newest-First", "Oldest-First"]:
    row = card_lookup[strategy]
    winner_class = " winner" if strategy == "Newest-First" else ""
    cards.append(
        f"""
        <div class="dashboard-card{winner_class}">
          <div class="dashboard-title">{strategy}</div>
          <div class="dashboard-value">{fmt_days(row["mttr_days"])} days</div>
          <div class="dashboard-label">MTTR</div>
        </div>
        """
    )

print('<div class="dashboard-grid">' + "".join(cards) + "</div>")
```

::: {.centered .muted}
This dashboard rewards the strategy that hides the backlog best.
:::

---

## Leadership sees the full system {auto-animate="true"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
cards = []
for strategy in ["Newest-First", "Oldest-First"]:
    row = card_lookup[strategy]
    card_class = "dashboard-card dashboard-detail"
    if strategy == "Oldest-First":
        card_class += " winner"
    cards.append(
        f"""
        <div class="{card_class}">
          <div class="dashboard-title">{strategy}</div>
          <div class="dashboard-stat">
            <span>MTTR</span>
            <strong>{fmt_days(row["mttr_days"])} days</strong>
          </div>
          <div class="dashboard-stat">
            <span>MOVA</span>
            <strong>{fmt_days(row["mova_days"])} days</strong>
          </div>
          <div class="dashboard-stat">
            <span>Open</span>
            <strong>{row["open_count"]}</strong>
          </div>
          <div class="dashboard-stat">
            <span>180+ days</span>
            <strong>{row["aged_over_180"]}</strong>
          </div>
        </div>
        """
    )

print('<div class="dashboard-grid">' + "".join(cards) + "</div>")
```

::: {.centered .muted}
MTTR still matters. It just stops being the only story.
:::

---

## One live view

```{python}
#| echo: false
ojs_define(
    metrics_js=(
        metrics_labeled
        .select(
            pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
            "month_index",
            "month_label",
            "mttr_days",
            "mova_days",
            "aged_over_180",
        )
        .to_dicts()
    ),
    summary_js=card_rows,
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selected_strategy = Inputs.radio(
  ["Oldest-First", "Newest-First"],
  {label: "Strategy", value: "Oldest-First"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const row = summary_js.find((d) => d.strategy === selected_strategy)
  return md`**Month 24:** MTTR **${row.mttr_days.toFixed(1)}** days, MOVA **${row.mova_days.toFixed(1)}** days, **${row.aged_over_180}** findings older than 180 days.`
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const rows = metrics_js.filter((d) => d.strategy === selected_strategy)
  const data = [
    ...rows.map((d) => ({month: d.month_index, days: d.mttr_days, metric: "MTTR"})),
    ...rows.map((d) => ({month: d.month_index, days: d.mova_days, metric: "MOVA"})),
  ]

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 900,
    height: 330,
    marginTop: 18,
    marginRight: 24,
    color: {
      legend: true,
      domain: ["MTTR", "MOVA"],
      range: ["#1F6F78", "#C65A21"],
    },
    x: {label: "Month", ticks: [1, 6, 12, 18, 24]},
    y: {label: "Days", grid: true},
    style: {
      background: "#fffaf2",
      color: "#182126",
      fontFamily: "Avenir Next, Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif",
      fontSize: "14px",
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.lineY(data, {x: "month", y: "days", stroke: "metric", strokeWidth: 4}),
      Plot.dot(data.filter((d) => d.month === 24), {x: "month", y: "days", fill: "metric", r: 5}),
      Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#d9cfbf"}),
    ],
  })
}
```

---

## Read the signals together

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR rising + MOVA falling = backlog cleanup
- MTTR falling + MOVA rising = optics improving while exposure worsens
- 180+ rising = risk getting stranded in the tail
:::

# Close {.center .section-slide}

## Keep both in the room {.center}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR tells you how finished work moved.

MOVA tells you how long risk stayed with you.
:::

---

## Final line {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
If your metrics punish
the right behavior,

teams will optimize
the wrong one.
:::
